decision_rule_or_boundary: Academic databases, citation indexes, arXiv, standards, technical reports, backward/forward citation tracing, and representative industrial systems were searched with five query families.
A source is retained when it clarifies a managed baseline, redistributed delivery function, retained coordination boundary, evidence form, stakeholder consequence, or failure mode.

Records are not cited when they duplicate retained sources, optimize conventional CDN behavior without moving a control boundary, provide less direct evidence, or fall outside the delivery-service boundary.
resulting_use: Keeps adjacent literature visible while preserving the comparison unit.

decision_rule_or_boundary: Historical anchors include classical CDN, P2P, ICN/NDN/CCN, DASH/ABR, QUIC/HTTP/3/MoQ, edge/MEC, CDN security, Web3 retrieval, and blockchain-assisted delivery.
resulting_use: Prevents the survey from being only a recent Web3/blockchain review.

decision_rule_or_boundary: Each source is coded for architecture family, redistributed function, affected plane, retained coordinator, evidence type, supported claim, stakeholder effect, and remaining evaluation focus.
resulting_use: Supports the responsibility-plane profiles and source-to-claim map.
